digest.top_line: NRG/RTOG 0848: adjuvant chemoRT misses OS overall (HR 0.96, p=0.38), but node-negative subgroup 5-yr OS doubles 28.6%→48.1% (interaction p=0.022).
digest.tldr: Sole signal of the day, from upper-GI: RTOG 0848's negative primary (chemoRT adds nothing to adjuvant gemcitabine on OS, HR 0.96) masks a node-negative subgroup where 5-yr OS nearly doubled (28.6%→48.1%, interaction p=0.022). Margin-positive pts saw no gain; hypothesis-generating, needs FOLFIRINOX-era confirmation.

sites.intro: Adjuvant pancreatic chemoRT stays unproven overall; the live RT question is whether node-negative pts are the subgroup that benefits.

studies.name: NRG Oncology/RTOG 0848
studies.tldr: Adjuvant chemoRT did not improve OS overall (HR 0.96, p=0.38); node-negative subgroup 5-yr OS 28.6%→48.1% (interaction p=0.022).
details: 📊 1° EP OS not improved by adding CXRT: median 2.3 vs 2.6 yr (Chemo+CXRT vs Chemo)

details.text: 📊 5-yr OS by pathologic N stage (Chemo vs Chemo+CXRT)

details: 📐 N-stage interaction p=0.022, the only significant treatment interaction across tested subgroups
🔍 RT randomization of RTOG 0848: N=354 randomized after gemcitabine-based adjuvant chemo (174 Chemo, 180 Chemo+CXRT)
🔍 CXRT = fluoropyrimidine-sensitized chemoradiotherapy; dose, fractionation, target volume not reported in source
🔗 Margin-positive pts (n=59) showed no CXRT gain: 5-yr OS 17.3%→14.3%, counter to RT-for-positive-margin rationale
⚠️ Gemcitabine-era backbone; node-negative signal untested vs modern FOLFIRINOX, needs independent confirmation

verdict.rationale: Prespecified primary endpoint (OS, all pts) negative, HR 0.96; reinforces chemo-alone practice. Node-negative subgroup benefit is exploratory (interaction p=0.022), needs independent confirmation.
verdict.audience: Resected pancreatic head adenocarcinoma, post-gemcitabine adjuvant chemo

studies.significance: The RT read is the node-negative subgroup (n=91), not the null primary: 5-yr OS 28.6% vs 48.1% with CXRT (interaction p=0.022), median 3.0 vs 3.9 yr. Omit adjuvant CXRT broadly, but node-negative resected pancreatic head is the population for a confirmatory RT trial. CXRT dose and target not in source text.
open_questions: Does node-negative CXRT benefit replicate with FOLFIRINOX backbone
RT dose, fractionation, and target volume for node-negative resected pancreatic head

related_trials.brief_title: Adjuvant Chemoradiotherapy Versus Chemotherapy for Pancreatic Cancer (ADJUPANC)
related_trials.brief_summary: In this trial, we aim to compare the outcomes of adjuvant chemoradiotherapy with chemotherapy for patients with resected pancreatic cancer who are at high risk of disease progressions.
related_trials.eligibility_brief:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Aged 18-75 years
* Pathologically confirmed pancreatic ductal adenocarcinoma
* Pathologically confirmed lymph node metastasis, R1 or R2 resection or lymphovascular invasion (one of the risk factors)
* No history of cancer treatment after surgical resection
* No disease…
related_trials.answers_question: Does node-negative CXRT benefit replicate with FOLFIRINOX backbone
related_trials.relevance_phrase: phase 3 adjuvant chemoRT vs chemo in resected PDAC

related_trials.brief_summary: While surgery is considered the only potentially curative therapy for pancreatic cancer, 5-year overall survival (OS) is typically \<25%. Following surgical resection of pancreatic cancer, adjuvant conventionally fractionated RT (CRT, delivering 45-54 Gy in 1.8-2.0 Gy per fraction) with 5-FU chemotherapy is recommended in high-risk patients (positive lymph nodes and/or R1-R2 resection margin status). However, the benefit of CRT in this setting is controversial due to lack of prospective…

* Age \> 18
